summary: The post argues that Apache Arrow will materially improve the experience of pandas users, especially when accessing data from Hadoop ecosystems. McKinney shows that current Hadoop-to-pandas pathways are far slower than local formats like HDF5 or even CSV, largely due to expensive serialization and Python object conversion. Arrow addresses these bottlenecks by providing a fast, columnar in-memory format that can flow directly into pandas at the C API level. He anticipates that Arrow-based ingestion will be faster than CSV and closer to native performance for big data systems like Spark. The conclusion is optimistic: Arrow’s impact will extend beyond access speed to better Python extensions and native handling of nested data, with Parquet support in C++ advancing toward pandas integration.
key_points: Hadoop-based data access for pandas is currently much slower than local binary formats, with serialization dominating execution time.
Two primary bottlenecks are costly deserialization formats and detours through scalar Python objects.
Apache Arrow enables zero-copy, columnar data transfer that should drastically improve pandas ingestion performance.
CSV is a poor performance baseline; binary columnar formats like HDF5 show the attainable speed gains.
Native Parquet support in C++ is progressing and is expected to bring near-term benefits to pandas users.
money_quotes: I'm super excited to be involved in the new open source Apache Arrow community initiative.
As the original author of both read_csv and HDFStore, these benchmarks make me very proud, but it also shows what level of data access performance pandas users should expect in general.
So more than 90% of the execution time is data serialization.
Apache Arrow helps mitigate both of these problems.
Realistically, the performance of ingesting data into pandas via Arrow should be significantly faster than reading a CSV (being binary and columnar).
Parsing a CSV is fairly expensive, which is why reading from HDF5 is 20x faster than parsing a CSV.
Data access performance is only one area where Arrow will help the Python ecosystem.
key_insight: Arrow’s columnar in-memory format removes serialization and Python object overheads, enabling pandas to access big data sources at near-native speeds and setting the stage for broader ecosystem gains.
